item.title: Amazon scraps AI leaderboard to stop workers chasing usage scores
item.description: Senior executive Dave Treadwell tells staff ‘don’t use AI just for the sake of using AI’ as costs rise

item.title: The chip and memory stock frenzy
item.description: As software lags, semiconductors catch up to the AI spending

item.title: Musk’s tweet undermines SpaceX’s claims about Anthropic data centre deal
item.description: Billionaire says the arrangement, described in IPO filings as a three-year agreement, only lasts for 180 days

item.title: Chevron CEO warns oil prices to jump over summer as supplies dwindle
item.description: Blockade in Strait of Hormuz has removed up to 13mn barrels a day from global markets and drawn down ‘shock absorbers’

item.title: Anthropic finalises $65bn funding deal to surpass OpenAI’s valuation
item.description: New round values Claude AI maker at $965bn including the new money

item.title: AkzoNobel should avoid painting itself into a corner
item.description: Dutch paint maker’s original tie-up with Axalta doesn’t look especially compelling

item.title: Caesars’ billionaire buyer hopes luck and leverage go hand in hand
item.description: The transaction comes at a time when Caesars and Las Vegas have been struggling

item.title: And just like that, the software apocalypse was over
item.description: Snowflake’s stock pop demonstrates what companies must do to escape the sector downturn

item.title: Iranian dissident news network received £650mn of debt relief
item.description: Finances of UK-based broadcaster Iran International, a leading platform for opposition to regime, shored up by shareholders

item.title: SpaceX IPO boldly steps into the unknown of AI economics
item.description: We still need to learn many things about the basic business model of frontier models

item.title: EU wants crisis powers to seize control of chip supplies
item.description: Chipmakers could be forced to override existing contracts under draft law

item.title: Germany shifts on EU oversight of financial services
item.description: Capital markets union ‘more important than clinging to national interests’, says finance minister

item.title: BP should never have made Manifold chair
item.description: The UK energy company’s board took an unwise gamble on a dominant executive

item.title: The Pope disrupts Silicon Valley
item.description: Unlike the US president, the pontiff is choosing to grapple with the serious challenges of AI

item.title: Ousted BP chair clashed with company secretary
item.description: Ben Mathews has taken time off work following upheaval at oil major

item.title: Billionaire Tilman Fertitta to buy Caesars Entertainment in $17.6bn deal
item.description: All-cash takeover will be one of the biggest gaming deals in years

item.title: Ex-SJP partners allege wealth manager took clients without paying
item.description: Former advisers preparing legal action over claims St James’s Place failed to pay compensation after taking their business

item.title: Who decides which jobs AI will take?
item.description: Different models are producing very different assessments of exposure levels

item.title: What made the DoJ go after this particular prediction market insider?
How Texas utilities are preparing for more extreme storms
item.description: The humble utility pole, a key part of the power distribution system, is getting an upgrade

item.title: EU fines China’s Temu €200mn for failing to prevent sale of illegal goods
item.description: Online retailer becomes second company to be punished under bloc’s Digital Services Act after Elon Musk’s X

item.title: Iraq faces summer blackouts
item.description: Iran war has exposed oil-rich nation’s dependence on Iranian gas for electricity as temperatures exceed 45C

item.title: FirstFT: Democrats’ hopes for victory in Texas rise after Paxton’s win
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US hits Iran with fresh strikes while Kirkland & Ellis develops its own AI platform

item.title: BDO fined £2mn for ‘pervasive breaches’ in audit of collapsed NMCN
item.description: UK regulator says mistakes in work for construction company were ‘significant and serious’

item.title: Ousted BP chair Albert Manifold hits out at ‘lies’ over his conduct
item.description: Executive says he ‘pushed hard’ at FTSE oil group but disputes criticism of his behaviour

item.title: Belgian gunmaker FN Browning to buy UK sniper producer
item.description: Acquisition comes as state-owned group looks to compete in British government rifle tender

item.title: Payments group accused of being ‘Chinese backdoor’ moves staff out of China
item.description: Australia-founded Airwallex is pursuing US expansion amid intensifying rivalry between Washington and Beijing

item.title: AI boom squeezes optical tech and Huawei makes a chip comeback
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Semiconductors: supercycle or superbubble?
item.description: Plus, semis’ fragile supply chain

item.title: FTAV’s further reading
item.description: The melancholy of slaying monsters; GCHQ; Maga hogs at the trough; volcanoes
